name: An attempt was made to reset an account's password
applicable_version: Windows Server 2008 R2 and later; Windows 7 and later
definition: Windows Security Event ID 4724 records an attempt to reset another account's password and is important for account-takeover investigations.
trigger_scenarios: The event is logged when an administrator, helpdesk workflow, or other principal resets a user password rather than changing its own password.

key_fields.explanation: The identity that attempted the reset. Confirm it is an approved administrator or identity-management process.
The account whose password was reset. Privileged, service, and recently created accounts deserve special attention.
The operation result. Failed resets can reveal attempted but blocked account abuse.

false_positives: Helpdesk and self-service identity workflows legitimately reset passwords.
Emergency access procedures may reset privileged accounts during incidents.
detection_notes: Prioritize resets of privileged or service accounts, resets performed by unusual actors, and reset events followed by new logons or group changes. Validate the ticket or workflow that authorized the action. Mapped ATT&CK techniques: T1098, T1078.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4724
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, TargetAccount, Activity, Status
| order by TimeGenerated desc
spl_snippet:
index=wineventlog EventCode=4724
| table _time, host, SubjectUserName, TargetUserName, Status
sample_log:
Subject: CORP\helpdesk01
Target Account: CORP\admin.ops
Status: 0x0
